examples.title: Sequential File Read Operation
examples.description: Demonstrates sequential access reading records one by one from the beginning
examples.code:
PROCEDURE SequentialRead(fileDescriptor)
  OPEN fileDescriptor FOR READING
  WHILE NOT EOF(fileDescriptor)
    READ record FROM fileDescriptor
    PROCESS record
  END WHILE
  CLOSE fileDescriptor
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: This algorithm reads records sequentially from the start until End of File is reached. Each record is processed in order.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is number of records

examples.title: Direct File Access Operation
examples.description: Demonstrates random/direct access to any record using seek
examples.code:
PROCEDURE DirectAccess(fileDescriptor, recordNumber, recordSize)
  OPEN fileDescriptor FOR RANDOM ACCESS
  offset = (recordNumber - 1) * recordSize
  SEEK fileDescriptor TO offset
  READ record FROM fileDescriptor
  PROCESS record
  CLOSE fileDescriptor
END PROCEDURE
examples.explanation: Uses seek operation to jump directly to the desired record position without reading preceding records.

examples.title: File Operations in Python
examples.description: Demonstrates basic file operations: create, write, read, seek
examples.code:
# File operations demonstration

# Create and open file for writing
file = open("data.txt", "w")
file.write("Hello, File System!")
file.close()

# Open file for reading
file = open("data.txt", "r")
content = file.read()
print(content)
file.close()

# Open with seek for random access
file = open("data.txt", "r+")
file.seek(6)  # Move to 7th character
data = file.read(5)  # Read 5 characters
print(data)
file.close()
examples.explanation: Shows Python's file handling: write mode creates/truncates, read mode reads content, seek enables random access positioning.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for read/write, O(1) for seek

examples.title: File Control Block Operations in C
examples.description: Demonstrates file operations using C standard library
examples.explanation: C uses FILE pointer as file descriptor. fprintf writes formatted data, fgets reads line by line, fseek positions the file pointer for direct access.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) for sequential, O(1) for fseek
